title: Next Greater Element I
description:
Given an array of integers nums, find the Next Greater Element for each element. The Next Greater Element of an element x is the first greater element to its right. If it does not exist, return -1.

Example:

Input: nums = [4, 2, 5, 1, 8]
Output: [5, 5, 8, 8, -1]
editorial:
Monotonic Stack Approach

This problem can be solved optimally in O(N) time using a Monotonic Decreasing Stack.

Algorithm:

  • Iterate through the array from left to right.
  • While the stack is not empty and the current element is greater than the element at the top of the stack, we have found the NGE for that index — pop and resolve it.
  • Push the current index onto the stack.

Complexity: Time O(N), Space O(N)

title: Daily Temperatures
description:
Given an array of integers temperatures representing the daily temperatures, return an array answer such that answer[i] is the number of days you have to wait after the i-th day to get a warmer temperature.

Example:

Input: [73, 74, 75, 71, 69, 72, 76, 73]
Output: [1, 1, 4, 2, 1, 1, 0, 0]
editorial:
Monotonic Stack Strategy

We traverse the temperatures and use a stack to keep track of indexes of days that have not yet seen a warmer temperature.

Complexity: Time O(N), Space O(N)

title: Binary Tree Preorder Traversal
description: Given the root of a binary tree, return the preorder traversal of its nodes' values. Preorder traversal visits the root first, then the left subtree, and finally the right subtree.
editorial:
Depth-First Search (DFS) Traversal

Preorder traversal visits nodes in the order: Root → Left → Right.

Complexity: Time O(N), Space O(H) where H is the height of the tree.

title: 2D Grid Island Traversal
description:
Given an m x n 2D binary grid which represents a map of 1s (land) and 0s (water), traverse all connected land cells using DFS.

An island is formed by connecting adjacent lands horizontally or vertically.
editorial:
DFS Grid Traversal

Whenever we encounter a land cell 1, we trigger a DFS to recursively visit all connected land cells.

Complexity: Time O(R × C), Space O(R × C)

title: Reverse Linked List
description:
Given the head of a singly linked list, reverse the list, and return the reversed list.

Example:

Input:  1 → 2 → 3 → 4 → 5
Output: 5 → 4 → 3 → 2 → 1
editorial:
Iterative Pointer Reversal

Maintain three pointers: prev, curr, and temp. At each step, reverse the next pointer of curr to point to prev, then advance all pointers.

Complexity: Time O(N), Space O(1)

title: Top K Elements
description: Given an integer array nums and an integer k, return the k largest elements in the array using a Max Priority Heap.
editorial:
Max Heap Extraction

Push all elements into a max heap. Then pop the top k times to extract the largest elements in order.

Complexity: Time O(N log N), Space O(N)

title: Two Sum
description:
Given an array of integers nums and an integer target, return indices of the two numbers such that they add up to target.

Example:

Input: nums = [2,7,11,15], target = 9
Output: [0, 1]
editorial:
Hash Map One-Pass

For each element, compute the complement (target - nums[i]) and check if it already exists in the hash map. If yes, we found our pair. Otherwise, store the current value.

Complexity: Time O(N), Space O(N)

title: Binary Search
description: Given an array of integers nums sorted in ascending order, and an integer target, write a function to search target in nums. If target exists, return its index. Otherwise, return -1.
editorial:
Divide and Conquer

Maintain two pointers: left and right. At each step, calculate the midpoint and discard half of the search range.

Complexity: Time O(log N), Space O(1)

title: Sliding Window Maximum
description: Given an array of integers nums, there is a sliding window of size k moving from left to right. Return the maximum element in the sliding window at each position.
editorial:
Monotonic Deque Approach

Maintain a Deque that stores indices of elements in the current window in decreasing order of values. The maximum is always at the front.

Complexity: Time O(N), Space O(K)

title: Valid Parentheses
description: Given a string containing just the characters (, ), {, }, [ and ], determine if the input string is valid. (In this visualizer, brackets are encoded as integers: 1=(, 2=), 3={, 4=}, 5=[, 6=].)
editorial:
Stack-Based Validation

Push opening brackets onto a stack. When encountering a closing bracket, verify it matches the top of the stack.

Complexity: Time O(N), Space O(N)

title: Adjacency Graph BFS
description: Given a graph defined by nodes and edges, perform a Breadth-First Search (BFS) to traverse all reachable nodes from a source node.
editorial:
Level-by-Level Queue Traversal

BFS uses a Queue to explore neighbors layer-by-layer. We track visited nodes to avoid revisiting.

Complexity: Time O(V + E), Space O(V)

title: Container With Most Water
description: Given n non-negative integers height representing vertical lines, find two lines that together with the x-axis form a container that stores the most water.
editorial:
Two Pointer Greedy Sweep

Place pointers at both ends. Calculate the area, then move the pointer pointing to the shorter line inward.

Complexity: Time O(N), Space O(1)
